title: takt init: commit scaffolded files to git after scaffold
description:
After 'takt init' runs scaffold_project(), the created files only exist in the working tree. When the scheduler later creates a git worktree via 'git worktree add', it checks out HEAD — which has none of the scaffolded files — so agents run without templates, skills, config, or specs structure.

Fix: add a git commit step at the end of scaffold_project() in onboarding.py.

Specific changes:

1. onboarding.py — add a commit_scaffold(project_root, console) helper that:
   - Adds .gitkeep files to empty directories that git would not otherwise track: .takt/beads/, specs/drafts/, specs/done/
   - Runs: git -C <project_root> add templates/ .agents/skills/ .claude/skills/ docs/memory/ specs/ .takt/config.yaml .takt/beads/.gitkeep .gitignore
   - Runs: git -C <project_root> commit -m 'chore: takt init scaffold'
   - If 'git add' or 'git commit' fails (e.g. nothing to stage, or already committed), logs a warning and continues — do not raise.
   - Uses subprocess.run with check=False; inspect returncode explicitly.

2. scaffold_project() — call commit_scaffold() as the final step (after step 7), printing success or warning via console.

Do NOT add a gitignore entry for .takt/beads/ — bead JSON files must be tracked so they are visible in worktrees. The existing gitignore entries (.takt/worktrees/, .takt/telemetry/, .takt/logs/, .takt/agent-runs/) are correct and should remain.
acceptance_criteria: 'takt init' on a fresh git repo ends with a git commit containing templates/, .agents/skills/, .claude/skills/, docs/memory/, specs/, .takt/config.yaml, and .takt/beads/.gitkeep
Running 'git log --oneline' after 'takt init' shows the scaffold commit
A git worktree created after 'takt init' contains all scaffolded files
If 'git commit' finds nothing to stage (re-running init with --overwrite and no changes), a warning is printed and the command exits 0
.takt/beads/ is NOT in .gitignore and bead JSON files are visible in worktrees
